zh.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith於大約1616年出生在英國諾福爾可辛加姆。他於大約1639年與Grace在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1634年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可馬布勒黑阿德克里夫頓且在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆住了大約2年。他於1636年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆被列為merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith.。他去世於1685年9月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦享年70歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦可福 波英 格洛德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith于大约1616年出生在英国諾福爾可辛加姆。他于大约1639年与Grace在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1634年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可馬布勒黑阿德克里夫頓且在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆住了大约2年。他于1636年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆被列为merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith.。他去世于1685年9月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦享年70岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦可福 波英 格洛德。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Oldest Grave seen in the Memorials List

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith was born about 1616, in Hingham, Norfolk, England. He married Grace about 1639, in Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He lived in Clifton, Marblehead, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1634 and Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America for about 2 years. In 1636, at the age of 23, his occupation is listed as merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith. in Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. He died on 14 September 1685, in Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 70, and was buried in Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, United States.

it.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith è nato il circa 1616 nel Hingham, Norfolk, England. Lui sposato Grace circa 1639, nel Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Vissuto a Clifton, Marblehead, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1634 e nel Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America per circa 2 anni. Nel 1636, all'età di 23 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith. nel Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Morì il 14 settembre 1685, nel Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph Smithは約1616年にイギリスノーフォルクヒンガムで生まれた。約1639年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヒンガムでGraceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1634年にはイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスマルブレヘドクリフトンに，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヒンガムに約2年間住んでいた。1636年，23歳の時，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヒンガムでの職業はmerchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith.と記録されている。彼は1685年9月14日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーバーンスタブルエアスサムで70歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州バーンスタブルエアスサムコブ ブリイング グロウンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith(은)는 대략 1616년 영국 노르폴크 잉함에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1639년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 잉함에서 Grace(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1634년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 마블레헤드 클리프턴에서, 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 잉함에서 약 2년 동안 살았다. 1636년, 23세의 나이로 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 잉함에서 merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith.이었다. 그는 1685년 9월 14일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 이스섬에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 바른스타블일 이스섬 커브 버이잉 그라운드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith nasceu aproximadamente 1616, em Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Grace aproximadamente 1639, em Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele viveu em Clifton, Marblehead, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1634 e em Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America por quase 2 anos. Em 1636, com 23 anos, sua profissão aparece como merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith. em Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1685, em Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith родился около 1616 г. в местности хингхам, норфолк, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace около 1639 г. в местности хингхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он жил в местности клифтон, марблэид, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1634 году и в местности хингхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика около 2 лет. В 1636 году, в возрасте 23 лет,  он работал по профессии merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith. в местности хингхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер 14 сентября 1685 г. в местности эастам, барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности ков буринг гроунд, эастам, барнстабл, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Ralph Smith nació aproximadamente 1616, en Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Se casó con Grace aproximadamente 1639, en Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Vivió en Clifton, Marblehead, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1634 y en Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America durante unos 2 años. En 1636, a la edad de 23 años, su ocupación registrada era merchant -  quoting the consolidated genealogies of the ralph smith family,  "it is said he had erected a store in hingham and left it in charge of josias hobart when he went to eastham, where he continued to trade with josias".   this same description is given in several sources for ralph smith. en Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1685, en Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
